figures.caption: Table 1: Comparison results on the GID24 (4 m) and GID24 (2 m) subsets. All models are trained exclusively on the 4 m training set, and the 2 m test set is utilized to evaluate generalization. The best results are highlighted in bold.

Figure 3: Qualitative comparison of segmentation results. The colored bounding boxes highlight complex regions where existingmethods suffer from severe fragmentation or boundary inconsistency, whereas our SSDMmaintains superior structural coherence and precise delineation.

Figure 2: Overview of the proposed SSDM framework. (a) Overall framework. The adapted global embeddings are functionally decoupled to guide the encoder via SMM and complement semantic information via SeMM. (b) Architecture of the SMM block. (c) Structural-aware self-attention mechanism.
Table 2: Per-class performance on the GID24 (4 m) subset.
